senses.definition: Fictional superhero who fights crime in Gotham City.
examples: Batman wears a black cape and mask.
Children love watching Batman movies.
Batman has a sidekick named Robin.

senses.definition: Officer’s servant in the British army, especially in the past.
examples: The batman cleaned the officer’s boots every morning.
In World War I stories, the batman helped with daily tasks.
He served as batman to a colonel for years.

senses.definition: Plural of batman, meaning officers’ servants in the army.
examples: The batmen attended to the needs of the officers.
During the campaign, batmen carried supplies.

senses.definition: Jewish ceremony for a girl aged 12 or 13 to celebrate becoming adult in the religion.
examples: Her batmitzvah was a big family celebration.
She read from the Torah at her batmitzvah.
The batmitzvah party lasted all night.

senses.definition: Plural of batmitzvah, Jewish coming-of-age ceremonies for girls.
examples: Many batmitzvahs happen in synagogues on Saturdays.
The community held three batmitzvahs that month.

senses.definition: Family of extinct crinoids, marine animals related to sea lilies.
examples: Batocrinidae fossils are found in ancient rock layers.
Paleontologists study Batocrinidae specimens.

senses.definition: Genus of fossil crinoids in the family Batocrinidae.
examples: Batocrinus species lived in ancient oceans.
A Batocrinus fossil was discovered in the quarry.

senses.definition: Genus of extinct plants or fossil plant structures.
examples: Batodendron remains are rare in the fossil record.

senses.definition: Relating to rays, skates, and similar flat fish with broad bodies.
examples: The batoid fish glided along the ocean floor.
Batoid species have cartilaginous skeletons.

senses.definition: Fish of the batoid group, like rays and skates.
examples: A batoid swam past the diver.
Batoids are common in tropical waters.

senses.definition: Suborder of cartilaginous fish including rays, skates, and guitarfish.
examples: Batoidei have flattened bodies adapted for bottom living.
Scientists classify stingrays in Batoidei.

senses.definition: Ethnic group or place name in southern Africa, often related to Batonga people.
examples: Batoka Gorge is near Victoria Falls.

senses.definition: Relating to batology, the study of bats.
examples: Batological research examines bat echolocation.

senses.definition: Plural of batology, studies or treatises on bats.
examples: Modern batologies use sonar technology.

senses.definition: Person who studies bats scientifically.
examples: The batologist tracked bats at night.
Batologists study bat migration patterns.

senses.definition: Plural of batologist, scientists who study bats.
examples: Batologists gathered for the conference.
Local batologists monitor cave populations.

senses.definition: Scientific study of bats.
examples: Batology reveals how bats navigate in the dark.
She specializes in batology.

senses.definition: Short, heavy stick carried by a police officer.
examples: The police officer held a baton.
He was hit with a baton during the protest.

senses.definition: Thin stick used by a conductor to direct an orchestra.
examples: The conductor waved his baton.
She dropped her baton during the performance.

senses.definition: Stick passed from one runner to another in a relay race.
examples: She passed the baton to her teammate.
The baton fell during the relay.

senses.definition: Past tense of baton, hit with a baton.
examples: The protester was batonned by police.
He was batonned during the riot.

senses.definition: Person who uses or twirls a baton, like in marching bands.
examples: The batoneer led the parade.
She trained to become a batoneer.

senses.definition: Ethnic group in Zambia and Zimbabwe, speaking a Bantu language.
examples: The Batonga people live along the Zambezi River.
Batonga music features drums.

senses.definition: Present participle of baton, hitting with a baton.
examples: Police were batoning the crowd.
He winced while batoning the door open.

senses.definition: Person skilled in using a baton, such as a conductor.
examples: The batonist directed the symphony flawlessly.

senses.definition: Relating to the art or style of baton use.
examples: Her batonistic movements were graceful.

senses.definition: In heraldry, marked with stripes or bands.
examples: The shield had a batonne pattern.

senses.definition: President or dean of a bar association, especially in French-speaking countries.
examples: The batonnier addressed the lawyers.
She was elected batonnier last year.
